agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics platform managing the lifecycle of parcel shipments from creation through delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers. The domain covers shipment state management and weight classification for billing purposes.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management — owns shipment lifecycle (creation, transit, delivery) and weight-based classification for charging.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A domain entity representing a parcel moving from a pickup address to a delivery address, with a defined weight and lifecycle state."},{"term":"Pickup Address","definition":"The origin location where a parcel is collected. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Delivery Address","definition":"The destination location where a parcel is to be delivered. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Shipment State","definition":"The current lifecycle phase of a shipment. Valid states: Pending, In Transit, Delivered."},{"term":"Pending","definition":"Initial state of a shipment after creation, before it begins transit."},{"term":"In Transit","definition":"State indicating the shipment is actively being transported. Irreversible — cannot return to Pending."},{"term":"Delivered","definition":"Terminal state indicating the shipment has reached its delivery address. No further state transitions allowed."},{"term":"Weight Bracket","definition":"A classification tier derived from shipment weight, used to determine the charging rate: Standard, Heavy, or Freight."},{"term":"Standard","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments 0–10 kg (boundary inclusion at exactly 10 kg is ambiguous)."},{"term":"Heavy","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments 10–50 kg (boundary inclusion at exactly 10 kg and 50 kg is ambiguous)."},{"term":"Freight","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments over 50 kg."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A shipment can only be created when both a pickup address and a delivery address are provided.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment without both endpoints is physically meaningless — there is no origin or destination for the courier. This is a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create a shipment missing either pickup address or delivery address MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A shipment in the In Transit state cannot transition back to Pending.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"State regression from In Transit to Pending would corrupt the shipment audit trail and violate the physical reality that a parcel already picked up cannot be un-picked-up.","testable_assertion":"Any state change request from In Transit to Pending MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A Delivered shipment is in a terminal state — no further state transitions are permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Delivery is a real-world finality event. Allowing state changes after delivery would break billing, proof-of-delivery records, and customer trust.","testable_assertion":"Any state change request on a shipment in Delivered state MUST be rejected regardless of target state."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment weight must be strictly positive (greater than zero).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A parcel with zero or negative weight is physically impossible and would break weight-bracket classification and billing.","testable_assertion":"Any shipment with weight <= 0 MUST be rejected at creation and at any weight update."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy ceiling likely driven by vehicle capacity or insurance limits. A VP could raise or lower this threshold based on fleet changes or new contracts.","testable_assertion":"Any shipment with weight > 1000 kg MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Weight bracket classification: 0–10 kg = Standard, 10–50 kg = Heavy, over 50 kg = Freight. Exact boundary behavior at 10 kg and 50 kg is unspecified.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Weight brackets are a pricing policy. Thresholds and tier names can be changed by commercial teams without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A shipment with weight 5 kg MUST be classified as Standard. A shipment with weight 25 kg MUST be classified as Heavy. A shipment with weight 75 kg MUST be classified as Freight."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The only valid state transitions are: Pending -> In Transit, In Transit -> Delivered.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is the implicit state machine derived from the stated constraints (no backward from transit, delivered is final). Any other transition would violate the physical shipment lifecycle.","testable_assertion":"Only Pending->InTransit and InTransit->Delivered transitions MUST be allowed; all other transitions MUST be rejected."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A user provides both a pickup address and a delivery address with a valid weight","when":"The user creates a shipment","then":"The shipment is created in Pending state with the correct weight bracket assigned"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A user provides only a pickup address (no delivery address)","when":"The user attempts to create a shipment","then":"The system rejects the creation with a validation error"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A user provides only a delivery address (no pickup address)","when":"The user attempts to create a shipment","then":"The system rejects the creation with a validation error"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A shipment exists in Pending state","when":"The shipment is moved to In Transit","then":"The state changes to In Transit successfully"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A shipment exists in In Transit state","when":"An attempt is made to move it back to Pending","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains In Transit"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A shipment exists in In Transit state","when":"The shipment is moved to Delivered","then":"The state changes to Delivered successfully"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A shipment exists in Delivered state","when":"An attempt is made to change its state to any other state","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains Delivered"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A shipment weight of 0 kg or negative","when":"The user attempts to create or update the shipment","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A shipment weight exceeding 1000 kg","when":"The user attempts to create or update the shipment","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A shipment with weight 5 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The shipment is classified as Standard"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A shipment with weight 25 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The shipment is classified as Heavy"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A shipment with weight 75 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The shipment is classified as Freight"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A shipment exists in Pending state","when":"An attempt is made to move it directly to Delivered","then":"The system rejects the transition (Pending can only go to In Transit)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Weight is exactly 10 kg","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — spec says '0-10 kg is standard' and '10-50 kg is heavy'. Could belong to either bracket. Requires product clarification on boundary inclusion (recommend: exclusive upper bound, so 10 kg = Heavy)."},{"scenario":"Weight is exactly 50 kg","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — spec says '10-50 kg is heavy' and 'over 50 kg is freight'. Could belong to either bracket. Requires product clarification (recommend: exclusive upper bound, so 50 kg = Freight)."},{"scenario":"Weight is exactly 1000 kg","expected_behavior":"Should be accepted — the rule says 'cannot exceed 1000 kg', meaning 1000 kg is the maximum allowed. Classified as Freight."},{"scenario":"Weight is a fractional value (e.g. 10.0001 kg)","expected_behavior":"Should be classified as Heavy since it exceeds 10 kg. Weight precision/rounding policy is not specified."},{"scenario":"Shipment created with missing or empty address strings","expected_behavior":"Should be rejected — an empty string is not a valid address. Spec requires a pickup address and delivery address to exist."},{"scenario":"Attempting Pending -> Pending (self-transition)","expected_behavior":"Should be rejected — only forward transitions are valid per the state machine."},{"scenario":"Delivered -> Delivered (self-transition)","expected_behavior":"Should be rejected — Delivered is terminal, no state changes of any kind."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Weight bracket misclassification at boundaries (10 kg, 50 kg) directly affects revenue. Misclassifying a Heavy shipment as Standard means undercharging; misclassifying Standard as Heavy means overcharging and customer disputes.","operational":"Clear state machine prevents shipment lifecycle corruption. Operations teams can trust that In Transit and Delivered states are reliable for dispatch and proof-of-delivery workflows.","user":"Customers need predictable pricing. Ambiguous weight boundaries could lead to inconsistent quotes. Clear state finality for Delivered gives customers confidence in delivery confirmation.","risk":"Primary risk is the undefined weight bracket boundaries leading to inconsistent billing across the platform. Secondary risk is weight precision handling — if not specified, rounding differences could shift shipments between brackets.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (shippers)","impact":"Pricing transparency depends on unambiguous weight bracket boundaries. Overcharging erodes trust; undercharging creates billing disputes later."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Revenue recognition depends on correct bracket classification. Boundary ambiguity could cause audit issues."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"State machine integrity ensures dispatchers can trust In Transit status and delivery confirmations."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Ambiguous bracket boundaries will generate support tickets from customers disputing their tier classification at exactly 10 or 50 kg."}]},"confidence":{"level":55,"reasoning":"The spec is largely clear on state transitions and weight constraints, but contains ambiguity at weight bracket boundaries (exactly 10 kg and 50 kg). These boundaries directly affect billing — a critical business concern. Confidence capped at 55 due to this unresolved ambiguity.","high_confidence_areas":["State machine transitions (Pending -> In Transit -> Delivered) are explicit and unambiguous","Terminal nature of Delivered state","Weight must be positive and <= 1000 kg","Shipment creation requires both addresses","Weight bracket classification for values clearly within a tier (e.g. 5 kg, 25 kg, 75 kg)"],"low_confidence_areas":["Weight precision and rounding policy not specified","Whether address validation goes beyond presence (e.g. format, geocoding)","Whether weight can be modified after shipment creation","What other shipment attributes exist beyond addresses and weight"],"assumptions":["Pending is the initial state for all newly created shipments","The state machine is linear: Pending -> In Transit -> Delivered with no skip or branch","Weight is assigned at creation time and the spec does not address mid-lifecycle weight changes","Addresses are validated for presence only — no format or geocoding requirements stated"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Weight bracket boundaries at exactly 10 kg and 50 kg are ambiguous. The spec states '0-10 kg is standard, 10-50 kg is heavy, over 50 kg is freight' — the value 10 kg could be Standard or Heavy, and 50 kg could be Heavy or Freight. This directly impacts billing correctness.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers (shippers)","Finance / Billing","Customer Support"],"options":["Inclusive lower bound / exclusive upper bound: [0,10) Standard, [10,50) Heavy, [50,1000] Freight — so 10 kg = Heavy, 50 kg = Freight","Inclusive upper bound / exclusive lower bound: (0,10] Standard, (10,50] Heavy, (50,1000] Freight — so 10 kg = Standard, 50 kg = Heavy","Ask PM to specify exact boundary behavior"],"recommendation":"Recommend option A (exclusive upper bound): Standard = (0, 10), Heavy = [10, 50), Freight = [50, 1000]. This is the most common convention in logistics rate cards and ensures the higher-rate bracket captures the boundary value, which is commercially safer."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this logistics domain description and produce a comprehensive domain analysis.

The description is clear and well-defined. Let me break it down:

1. Shipment creation requires pickup + delivery address
2. State transitions: pending -> in_transit -> delivered (no backward from in_transit, delivered is final)
3. Weight: positive, max 1000kg
4. Weight brackets: 0-10 standard, 10-50 heavy, 50+ freight

No contradictions, no out-of-scope. Some minor ambiguities around edge cases (exact boundary values for weight brackets - is 10kg standard or heavy? is 50kg heavy or freight?). 

The weight bracket boundaries are genuinely ambiguous—10kg and 50kg could fall into either adjacent category. This is the primary blocker, so I'm classifying it as an ambiguity issue with confidence at or below 55.
